title: ServiceNow Inc (NOW) Draws Analyst Attention Amid AI Shift
detail:
ServiceNow Inc (NOW) Draws Analyst Attention Amid AI Shift
Abdul Rahman
Mon, February 16, 2026 at 10:05 AM EST 2 min read
In this article:
NOW
MSFT
INTU
CRM
TEAM

ServiceNow Inc (NYSE:NOW) is one of the best NYSE stocks to buy for the long term. On February 11, ServiceNow, Inc. (NYSE:NOW) announced the appointment of Danielle Fontaine as chief accounting officer and corporate controller, effective February 17. The update was shared in a press release following a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

ServiceNow Inc (NOW) Draws Analyst Attention Amid AI Shift

Earlier, on February 9, Morgan Stanley said the sharp drop in software valuations has created buying opportunities, arguing that worries about generative AI disruption are overdone. Analyst Keith Weiss noted software multiples have fallen about 33% since October 2025, with investors underestimating how established vendors can benefit from AI.

The firm highlighted Microsoft, Intuit, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Atlassian, Snowflake, Cloudflare, Shopify, and Palo Alto Networks as attractive picks, citing strong product cycles, better financials, and cheaper valuations.

Separately, on February 5, Truist cut its price target on ServiceNow to $175 from $240 but kept a Buy rating. The firm said the recent pullback in infrastructure software stocks is mostly about long‑term value concerns, not near‑term fundamentals, making AI stories more important.

Truist noted that companies relying on seat‑based models performed the worst in 2025 and continue to lag in early 2026. The analysts see growing traction for AI use cases and believe shifting away from seat‑based deployments is becoming a key strategy for these vendors.

ServiceNow Inc (NYSE:NOW) stock is trading near its 52-week low. This is despite the company posting stellar Q4 2025 earnings on January 28. Revenue rose 20.5% YoY to $3.57 billion, powered by a strong subscription business. On the back of the strong earnings, ServiceNow announced a $5 billion boost to its share repurchase program.

ServiceNow Inc (NYSE:NOW) is an American software company that provides a cloud-based and AI-driven platform for automating and improving business workflows. Its solutions help businesses increase productivity and maximize outcomes. Its primary operating areas are CRM and Industry, Technology, Core Business, and Creator.

While we acknowledge the potential of NOW as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: Cloudflare (NET) Is Up 12.7% After Strong Q4, Upbeat 2026 Outlook and Rising AI Demand – What's Changed
detail:
Cloudflare (NET) Is Up 12.7% After Strong Q4, Upbeat 2026 Outlook and Rising AI Demand – What's Changed
Simply Wall St
Mon, February 16, 2026 at 9:13 AM EST 3 min read
In this article:
NET

Cloudflare recently reported fourth-quarter 2025 results, with revenue rising to US$614.51 million and quarterly net loss narrowing slightly compared with the prior year.

The company also issued higher-than-expected 2026 revenue guidance and highlighted growing AI-related usage of its network, while long-serving Chief Legal Officer Doug Kramer plans to transition to a senior advisory role.

Next, we’ll explore how Cloudflare’s stronger-than-expected revenue outlook and AI-driven demand influence the existing investment narrative for the company.

The future of work is here. Discover the 32 top robotics and automation stocks leading the charge in AI-driven automation and industrial transformation.

Cloudflare Investment Narrative Recap

To own Cloudflare, I think you need to believe it can convert its global network and rising AI-related traffic into a durable, profitable software and security platform, despite current losses and intense hyperscaler competition. The latest earnings beat and stronger 2026 revenue guidance appear to support the near term revenue growth catalyst, while margin pressure and ongoing investment still leave profitability timing and valuation as the key risks that this update does not fully resolve.

Among the recent announcements, the 2026 revenue guidance of US$2,785.0 million to US$2,795.0 million looks most relevant, because it directly ties into the growth story investors are watching. It frames how quickly Cloudflare is trying to scale into its AI and security opportunity, but also sharpens the focus on whether that growth can eventually be delivered with healthier margins, given the company’s current unprofitable position and elevated price to sales multiple.

Yet behind Cloudflare’s upbeat revenue outlook, tighter data rules and rising compliance costs could become a more serious headwind than many investors currently realize...

title: Here is What to Know Beyond Why Cloudflare, Inc. (NET) is a Trending Stock
detail:
Here is What to Know Beyond Why Cloudflare, Inc. (NET) is a Trending Stock
Zacks Equity Research
Mon, February 16, 2026 at 9:00 AM EST 5 min read
In this article:
NET
^GSPC

Cloudflare (NET) is one of the stocks most watched by Zacks.com visitors lately. So, it might be a good idea to review some of the factors that might affect the near-term performance of the stock.

Shares of this web security and content delivery company have returned +6.3% over the past month versus the Zacks S&P 500 composite's -1.7% change. The Zacks Internet - Software industry, to which Cloudflare belongs, has lost 9.6% over this period. Now the key question is: Where could the stock be headed in the near term?

Although media reports or rumors about a significant change in a company's business prospects usually cause its stock to trend and lead to an immediate price change, there are always certain fundamental factors that ultimately drive the buy-and-hold decision.

Revisions to Earnings Estimates

Rather than focusing on anything else, we at Zacks prioritize evaluating the change in a company's earnings projection. This is because we believe the fair value for its stock is determined by the present value of its future stream of earnings.

We essentially look at how sell-side analysts covering the stock are revising their earnings estimates to reflect the impact of the latest business trends. And if earnings estimates go up for a company, the fair value for its stock goes up. A higher fair value than the current market price drives investors' interest in buying the stock, leading to its price moving higher. This is why empirical research shows a strong correlation between trends in earnings estimate revisions and near-term stock price movements.

For the current quarter, Cloudflare is expected to post earnings of $0.23 per share, indicating a change of +43.8% from the year-ago quarter. The Zacks Consensus Estimate has changed -185.2% over the last 30 days.

For the current fiscal year, the consensus earnings estimate of $1.11 points to a change of +19.4% from the prior year. Over the last 30 days, this estimate has changed -355.8%.

For the next fiscal year, the consensus earnings estimate of $1.43 indicates a change of +28.4% from what Cloudflare is expected to report a year ago. Over the past month, the estimate has changed -8.9%.

Having a strong externally audited track record, our proprietary stock rating tool, the Zacks Rank, offers a more conclusive picture of a stock's price direction in the near term, since it effectively harnesses the power of earnings estimate revisions. Due to the size of the recent change in the consensus estimate, along with three other factors related to earnings estimates, Cloudflare is rated Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell).

title: Goldman Launches AI-Resilient Trade Basket
detail:
Goldman Launches AI-Resilient Trade Basket
Moz Farooque ACCA
Mon, February 16, 2026 at 6:52 AM EST 1 min read
In this article:
GS
CRM
ACN
DOCU
DUOL

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Goldman Sachs (GS) is making a pretty bold call on software. The firm has rolled out a new pair trade basket that goes long on companies it believes are positioned to benefit from AI, and short on those it thinks could be disrupted by it.

On the long side, Goldman is leaning into infrastructure and cybersecurity names like Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Oracle (NYSE:ORCL), Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ:PANW), CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) and Cloudflare (NYSE:NET). These are companies building the plumbing or protecting the pipes of the AI economy. On the short side, it flagged Salesforce (NYSE:CRM), Accenture (NYSE:ACN), DocuSign (NASDAQ:DOCU), Monday.com (NASDAQ:MNDY) and Duolingo (NASDAQ:DUOL), arguing their services may be more exposed to automation risk.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 6 Warning Signs with ORCL.

Is ORCL fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The backdrop is tough. The SPDR S&P Software ETF (XSW) is down about 19% year to date, while the S&P 500 is roughly flat. Goldman's team expects a sharper split in performance as the dust settles, with AI builders separating from potential AI casualties.

title: Cloudflare’s Record Quarter Puts AI Edge Growth And Governance In Focus
detail:
Cloudflare’s Record Quarter Puts AI Edge Growth And Governance In Focus
Simply Wall St
Mon, February 16, 2026 at 1:05 AM EST 4 min read
In this article:
NET

Find your next quality investment with Simply Wall St's easy and powerful screener, trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

Cloudflare (NYSE:NET) reported its strongest quarter of 2025, with revenue above expectations and faster growth in both revenue and billings.

The company secured its largest contract to date, adding a new long term customer commitment to its platform.

Management highlighted rising demand for Cloudflare’s edge infrastructure as customers look to run artificial intelligence agents closer to users.

Cloudflare shares recently traded at $195.85, with the stock up 13.1% over the past week and 14.4% over the past year. Over a 3 year period, NYSE:NET has returned 203.9% and 138.3% over 5 years. This puts the latest quarter in the context of an already strong multi year share move.

For you as an investor, the key questions now are how durable this AI related demand could be and how much of it Cloudflare can capture with its edge network. The company’s recent results and record deal provide additional data points to watch as the market reassesses what long term growth might look like for NYSE:NET.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Cloudflare by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Cloudflare.

NYSE:NET 1-Year Stock Price Chart

Does the team leading Cloudflare have what it takes? See our full breakdown of the management team's track record and compensation.

For Cloudflare, this strong quarter arrives alongside an important leadership transition. Long serving Chief Legal Officer Doug Kramer plans to step down at the end of March 2026 after nearly a decade, moving into a Senior Advisor role, while Deputy Chief Legal Officer and Global Head of Public Policy Alissa Starzak is expected to take over. For you, the key question is whether this affects execution as Cloudflare leans harder into AI infrastructure and larger enterprise deals. The legal and policy team sits at the heart of issues like data localization, AI regulation and large contract negotiations with customers that may span multiple jurisdictions. A smooth handover, helped by Kramer staying on as advisor, can support continuity just as Cloudflare is talking up demand for AI agents on its edge network and backing that up with its largest contract to date.

How This Fits Into The Cloudflare Narrative

The leadership change in the legal and policy function may support the narrative that Cloudflare is positioning around AI, security and zero trust, as regulatory and contractual expertise is central to scaling those areas with large enterprises and governments.

At the same time, any disruption in a key executive role could challenge expectations that Cloudflare smoothly executes on high value, multi product deals, especially where complex compliance and data residency requirements are involved.

The narrative focuses heavily on AI driven growth and new business models, while this transition in legal leadership highlights an additional execution factor that might not be fully reflected, particularly around future AI and data regulation.

title: Cloudflare (NET) Draws Fresh Optimism After Earnings Beat, KeyBanc Reiterates Overweight

Cloudflare (NET) Draws Fresh Optimism After Earnings Beat, KeyBanc Reiterates Overweight
Ghazal Ahmed
Sat, February 14, 2026 at 9:23 AM EST 1 min read
In this article:
NET

Cloudflare, Inc. (NYSE:NET) is one of the AI stocks currently on Wall Street’s radar.

Delivering its “best quarter of 2025,” an AI stock on Wall Street’s radar post earnings has been Cloudflare. On February 11, KeyBanc analyst Jackson Ader reiterated an Overweight rating and $300.00 price target.

According to the firm, Cloudflare exceeded the firm’s estimates by over $24 million, a 4.2% beat against expectations. It was another quarter of acceleration, noted the firm, demonstrating continued acceleration in its growth momentum.

The company’s guidance for 2026 reflects almost 29% growth at the midpoint, with the firm suggesting that acceleration to 30% growth in 2026 is “plenty achievable.”

Cloudflare (NET) Draws Fresh Optimism After Earnings Beat, KeyBanc Reiterates Overweight

KeyBanc further noted that Cloudflare is expected to continue its acceleration into 2026, with its revenue momentum keeping it Overweight on the stock.

“On a full-year basis, NET is set to continue to accelerate in 2026 to over 30% with net-new ACV growing nearly 50% in 4Q25. It was the highest growth since 2021 and was aided by the largest ACV deal in history. Momentum in revenue keeps us Overweight on the stock.”

Cloudflare, Inc. (NYSE:NET) is a cloud services provider that powers AI workloads through its Workers AI platform.

While we acknowledge the potential of NET as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. That said, this earnings season is showing that the major players in the AI trade are posting strong numbers and reiterating or increasing their guidance. Ultimately, earnings are the tell when it comes to stock prices, which is why the overall outlook for stocks remains bullish.

title: Stock Market Today, Feb. 13: Fastly Rallies on Earnings Turnaround and Expanding AI Edge Strategy
detail:
Stock Market Today, Feb. 13: Fastly Rallies on Earnings Turnaround and Expanding AI Edge Strategy
Eric Trie, The Motley Fool
Fri, February 13, 2026 at 5:21 PM EST 2 min read
In this article:
FSLY
^GSPC
AKAM
^IXIC
NET

Fastly (NASDAQ:FSLY), an edge cloud platform for secure, scalable digital content delivery, closed Friday at $18.26, up 13.84%. The stock is advancing as investors react to record Q4 results, a surprise AI cloud partnership, while watching how agentic AI demand sustains revenue and margin momentum. The company’s trading volume reached 55 million shares, about 710% above its three-month average of 6.8 million shares.

How the markets moved today

S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) finished Friday fractionally higher at 6,836, up 0.05%, while the Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX: ^IXIC) slipped 0.22% to 22,547. Within internet services & infrastructure, peers Cloudflare (NYSE:NET) closed at $195.85, up 5.87%, and Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) ended at $111.76, rising 6.83%, as investors lean into AI-related traffic opportunities.

What this means for investors

Fastly shares rose sharply on Friday after the company reported record fourth-quarter results and issued stronger-than-expected 2026 guidance, signaling a potential turning point in growth and profitability. Revenue grew 23% year over year to $172.6 million, adjusted EPS reached $0.12, and GAAP losses narrowed. This indicates that operating leverage is beginning to take effect after several years of inconsistent performance.

The company’s 2026 revenue outlook of $700 million to $720 million suggests recent gains is likely to be sustainable. Management’s focus on AI cloud partnerships and agentic AI workloads is shifting market perception of Fastly’s role in digital infrastructure. The company is now increasingly viewed as a beneficiary of AI-driven edge traffic growth rather than a traditional content-delivery turnaround. The sustainability of this re-rating will depend heavily on continued growth in AI-related traffic, which will support ongoing revenue and margin improvement through 2026.

title: Analysts Have Made A Financial Statement On Cloudflare, Inc.'s (NYSE:NET) Yearly Report
detail:
Analysts Have Made A Financial Statement On Cloudflare, Inc.'s (NYSE:NET) Yearly Report
Simply Wall St
Fri, February 13, 2026 at 5:38 AM EST 3 min read
In this article:
NET

Shareholders of Cloudflare, Inc. (NYSE:NET) will be pleased this week, given that the stock price is up 14% to US$185 following its latest full-year results. Cloudflare reported revenues of US$2.2b, in line with expectations, but it unfortunately also reported (statutory) losses of US$0.29 per share, which were slightly larger than expected. Earnings are an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance, look at what the analysts are forecasting for next year, and see if there's been a change in sentiment towards the company. With this in mind, we've gathered the latest statutory forecasts to see what the analysts are expecting for next year.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

NYSE:NET Earnings and Revenue Growth February 13th 2026

Taking into account the latest results, the consensus forecast from Cloudflare's 31 analysts is for revenues of US$2.80b in 2026. This reflects a huge 29% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share losses are predicted to creep up to US$0.30. Yet prior to the latest earnings, the analysts had been forecasting revenues of US$2.74b and losses of US$0.16 per share in 2026. So it's pretty clear the analysts have mixed opinions on Cloudflare even after this update; although they upped their revenue numbers, it came at the cost of a regrettable increase in per-share losses.

View our latest analysis for Cloudflare

The consensus price target stayed unchanged at US$235, seeming to suggest that higher forecast losses are not expected to have a long term impact on the valuation. The consensus price target is just an average of individual analyst targets, so - it could be handy to see how wide the range of underlying estimates is. There are some variant perceptions on Cloudflare, with the most bullish analyst valuing it at US$300 and the most bearish at US$135 per share. This is a fairly broad spread of estimates, suggesting that analysts are forecasting a wide range of possible outcomes for the business.

Looking at the bigger picture now, one of the ways we can make sense of these forecasts is to see how they measure up against both past performance and industry growth estimates. We can infer from the latest estimates that forecasts expect a continuation of Cloudflare'shistorical trends, as the 29% annualised revenue growth to the end of 2026 is roughly in line with the 29% annual growth over the past five years. By contrast, our data suggests that other companies (with analyst coverage) in a similar industry are forecast to see their revenues grow 12% per year. So it's pretty clear that Cloudflare is forecast to grow substantially faster than its industry.

title: Coinbase suffers trading disruption on earnings day
detail:
Coinbase suffers trading disruption on earnings day
Anand Sinha
Thu, February 12, 2026 at 4:36 PM EST 1 min read
In this article:
NET

Trading on Coinbase Global (Nasdaq: COIN), the largest crypto trading exchange in the U.S., got disrupted on Feb. 12.

"We are aware that customers may be unable to buy, sell, transfer on Coinbase.com at this time. Our team is investigating this issue and will provide an update. Your funds are safe," Coinbase informed the users on X at 13:11 pm.

After more than an hour, the exchange shared at 14:31 pm that the issue had been resolved. It didn't reveal the reason behind the disruption.

Related: JPMorgan cuts Coinbase's price target ahead of earnings

This isn't the first time that the popular crypto exchange has faced trading disruption.

On Oct. 20 last year, users couldn't access Coinbase due to an Amazon Web Services (AWS) outage.

On Dec. 5 again, users couldn't access Coinbase and other centralized crypto platforms for some time due to a Cloudflare outage.

Though the crypto industry began on the promise of decentralization, several crypto exchanges like Coinbase run on centralized servers like AWS and Cloudflare.

While these centralized exchanges offer easy user interface and successfully attract new users, the trade-off often comes in the form of service disruptions due to server failures.

Decentralized crypto exchanges, on the other hand, are comparatively not so dependent on centralized systems but their highly technical nature ends up turning away users who might not very technically proficient.

More News:

Major crypto firm files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy

Coinbase secures major legal win in banking lawsuit

Analyst trims Coinbase price target after market wipeout

Coinbase to report Q4 2025 earnings today

Coinbase will report its earnings for the fourth quarter of 2025 after the closing bell today.

The COIN stock was trading more than 7% lower at $142.25 at the time of writing.

Related: Dogecoin founder sends harsh response to Elon Musk over X algorithm

title: Is Cloudflare (NET) a Buy as Wall Street Analysts Look Optimistic?
detail:
Is Cloudflare (NET) a Buy as Wall Street Analysts Look Optimistic?
Zacks Equity Research
Thu, February 12, 2026 at 9:30 AM EST 4 min read
In this article:
NET

When deciding whether to buy, sell, or hold a stock, investors often rely on analyst recommendations. Media reports about rating changes by these brokerage-firm-employed (or sell-side) analysts often influence a stock's price, but are they really important?

Before we discuss the reliability of brokerage recommendations and how to use them to your advantage, let's see what these Wall Street heavyweights think about Cloudflare (NET).

Cloudflare currently has an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.97, on a scale of 1 to 5 (Strong Buy to Strong Sell), calculated based on the actual recommendations (Buy, Hold, Sell, etc.) made by 33 brokerage firms. An ABR of 1.97 approximates between Strong Buy and Buy.

Of the 33 recommendations that derive the current ABR, 18 are Strong Buy and two are Buy. Strong Buy and Buy respectively account for 54.6% and 6.1% of all recommendations.

Brokerage Recommendation Trends for NET
Broker Rating Breakdown Chart for NET

Check price target & stock forecast for Cloudflare here>>>

The ABR suggests buying Cloudflare, but making an investment decision solely on the basis of this information might not be a good idea. According to several studies, brokerage recommendations have little to no success guiding investors to choose stocks with the most potential for price appreciation.

Do you wonder why? As a result of the vested interest of brokerage firms in a stock they cover, their analysts tend to rate it with a strong positive bias. According to our research, brokerage firms assign five "Strong Buy" recommendations for every "Strong Sell" recommendation.

In other words, their interests aren't always aligned with retail investors, rarely indicating where the price of a stock could actually be heading. Therefore, the best use of this information could be validating your own research or an indicator that has proven to be highly successful in predicting a stock's price movement.

With an impressive externally audited track record, our proprietary stock rating tool, the Zacks Rank, which classifies stocks into five groups, ranging from Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) to Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell), is a reliable indicator of a stock's near-term price performance. So, validating the Zacks Rank with ABR could go a long way in making a profitable investment decision.

Zacks Rank Should Not Be Confused With ABR

In spite of the fact that Zacks Rank and ABR both appear on a scale from 1 to 5, they are two completely different measures.

Broker recommendations are the sole basis for calculating the ABR, which is typically displayed in decimals (such as 1.28). The Zacks Rank, on the other hand, is a quantitative model designed to harness the power of earnings estimate revisions. It is displayed in whole numbers -- 1 to 5.

Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

Cloudflare (NYSE:NET) announced the acquisition of AI platform Replicate and another company to expand its developer and AI capabilities.

The company also reported its largest annual contract value to date, with record enterprise and AI focused deals.

Management highlighted these developments as reinforcing Cloudflare's role in infrastructure for an automated, AI driven internet.

Cloudflare runs a global network that powers security, performance, and developer services for internet applications, with an increasing focus on AI workloads. The Replicate acquisition plugs directly into that developer story, giving builders access to AI tooling on top of Cloudflare's existing infrastructure.

For investors, the combination of record annual contract value and targeted AI acquisitions presents a clearer narrative about where NYSE:NET is trying to compete against large cloud providers. How effectively Cloudflare converts these larger enterprise and AI relationships into durable, long term contracts will be a key factor to monitor over coming quarters.

detail:

Wed, February 11, 2026 at 7:21 PM EST 3 min read
In this article:
NET

Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

What Cloudflare’s latest earnings and AI momentum mean for the stock

Cloudflare (NET) just posted fourth quarter results that topped analyst expectations, highlighted record annual contract value tied to AI demand, and paired that with a positive 2026 revenue outlook that pushed the stock sharply higher.

See our latest analysis for Cloudflare.

Cloudflare’s share price has reacted strongly to this earnings news and upbeat 2026 revenue guidance, with a 7 day share price return of 13.5% and a 1 year total shareholder return of 9.6%. The 3 year total shareholder return of about 2.6x points to longer term momentum, despite a 90 day share price return of 11.3% and year to date share price return of 3.4%.

If Cloudflare’s AI story has caught your attention, it could be a good moment to see how other names stack up using our screener of 34 AI infrastructure stocks as potential ideas to research next.

With the shares up sharply and Cloudflare trading at a premium to many software peers, the key question for investors is simple: is this justifiably expensive growth, or could the recent AI fueled surge still leave a buying opportunity the market has not fully priced in?

Most Popular Narrative: 18.6% Undervalued

Cloudflare’s most followed narrative suggests a fair value above the last close of $189.41, with AI, security and zero trust at the center of the story.

The accelerating adoption of AI, explosion in global web traffic, and proliferation of IoT devices are driving increased demand for fast, secure, and resilient cloud-native infrastructure, which is Cloudflare's core strength, evidenced by strategic partnerships with major AI companies and record-breaking DDoS mitigation, positioning the company for sustained top-line revenue growth and strengthening customer retention.

Read the complete narrative.

Want to see what is actually baked into that valuation call? The narrative leans on ambitious revenue growth, rising margins and a premium future earnings multiple. Curious how those ingredients fit together?

Result: Fair Value of $232.78 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, that upside view could be challenged if customer concentration bites, or if competition and pricing pressure keep weighing on gross margins and delay profitability.

Find out about the key risks to this Cloudflare narrative.

Another lens on valuation

That 18.6% “undervalued” fair value sits oddly next to how the market is pricing Cloudflare today. On a P/S of 33.1x versus a fair ratio of 13.6x, peer average of 13.3x, and US IT around 2x, the gap points to meaningful valuation risk if sentiment cools.

title: Is It Too Late To Consider Cloudflare (NET) After Recent Internet Security Headlines?
detail:
Is It Too Late To Consider Cloudflare (NET) After Recent Internet Security Headlines?
Simply Wall St
Wed, February 11, 2026 at 5:08 PM EST 6 min read
In this article:
NET

Find your next quality investment with Simply Wall St's easy and powerful screener, trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

If you are looking at Cloudflare and wondering whether the current share price really lines up with the business, this article will walk through what the numbers are actually saying about value.

The stock recently closed at US$179.98, with returns of 5.7% over the past week, a 1.5% decline over the last 30 days, an 8.2% decline year to date, 4.3% over the past year, and a very large gain over three and five years.

Recent headlines around Cloudflare have focused on its role in internet security and performance, including coverage of its position as a key infrastructure provider for web applications and content delivery. This context helps frame why the share price has been moving as investors weigh that role against expectations already embedded in the stock.

Right now, Cloudflare scores 0 out of 6 on our valuation checks, which you can see in detail in our valuation score. Next we will compare what different valuation approaches say about the stock before looking at a more complete way to think about value at the end of the article.

Cloudflare scores just 0/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Approach 1: Cloudflare Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to the present. It is essentially asking what all those future dollars are worth in today’s terms.

For Cloudflare, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $269.4 million. Analyst and extrapolated estimates then step this forward, with projected free cash flow of $1.47b in 2030 and a series of annual projections in between, all expressed in $ and discounted back to today.

When you total those discounted cash flows, Simply Wall St’s DCF output suggests an intrinsic value of about $85.42 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$179.98, the model indicates Cloudflare is around 110.7% overvalued on this cash flow view. For investors who focus on cash generation, this implies a lot of optimism is already reflected in the current price.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Cloudflare may be overvalued by 110.7%. Discover 51 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.
